title: Compilation error
author: yexua

title: Support multimodal content parts in chat completion tool messages
author: michaelfeil
body:
`async-openai` currently models `ChatCompletionRequestToolMessageContentPart` as text-only. That matches some OpenAPI/spec wording, but real clients send multimodal tool-message content arrays, and the OpenAI hosted API accepts them.

Would like to contribute a fix here.

Example payload shape:

```json
{
  "role": "tool",
  "tool_call_id": "call_123",
  "content": [
    { "type": "text", "text": "Captured screenshot:" },
    {
      "type": "image_url",
      "image_url": {
        "url": "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo=",
        "detail": "low"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

A similar shape exists for audio:

```json
{
  "role": "tool",
  "tool_call_id": "call_456",
  "content": [
    { "type": "text", "text": "Captured audio:" },
    {
      "type": "input_audio",
      "input_audio": {
        "data": "UklGRiQ=",
        "format": "wav"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Current behavior

Deserialization fails because `ChatCompletionRequestToolMessageContentPart` only accepts `text`.

This blocks agentic/tool workflows where a tool returns screenshots, audio clips, or other multimodal observations back into the conversation.

## Expected behavior

`ChatCompletionRequestToolMessageContentPart` should accept the same multimodal content part variants used elsewhere in chat messages, at least:

- `text`
- `image_url`
- `input_audio`

Potentially this can reuse the existing content part structs:

- `ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartText`
- `ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartImage`
- `ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartAudio`

## Why this matters

Several agent frameworks and customer applications use tool messages to return observations from tools, including screenshot/capture tools. The tool result is semantically a tool message, not a user message, so rewriting it into a user message is awkward and can change the conversation structure.

Even if the OpenAPI schema says tool messages are text-only, the hosted API appears to be more permissive, and strict deserialization in the client library makes it impossible to proxy or handle these valid-in-practice requests.

## Suggested implementation

Extend the enum roughly like:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Serialize, Clone, PartialEq)]
#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum ChatCompletionRequestToolMessageContentPart {
    Text(ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartText),
    ImageUrl(ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartImage),
    InputAudio(ChatCompletionRequestMessageContentPartAudio),
}
```

And keep:

```rust
#[serde(untagged)]
pub enum ChatCompletionRequestToolMessageContent {
    Text(String),
    Array(Vec<ChatCompletionRequestToolMessageContentPart>),
}
```

Happy to open a PR with tests for text string, text array, `image_url`, `input_audio`, and full `ChatCompletionRequestMessage::Tool` deserialization.

title: `CreateResponses` requires `id` field when OpenAI itself does not require it
author: howardjohn

title: Support for GPT-Image-2
author: YourCarma
body:
**Hello!**
**_Thank you_** for this crate - it's very useful for me :)
Have you got plans for support `GPT-Image-2` model and sizes? Now, I need to use `Other` model for OpenAI Provider - OpenAI API return to me unknown for crate size:
```error
2026-06-02T07:32:56.076516Z ERROR tokio-rt-worker ThreadId(06) request{method=POST uri=/api/v1/images/generate/file version=HTTP/1.1}: mage_generation::server::errors: Error: Another Error: Error on Deserialize request: context: unknown variant `1254x1254`, expected one of `auto`, `256x256`, `512x512`, `1024x1024`, `1792x1024`, `1024x1792`, `1536x1024`, `1024x1536`
```

Actual crate OpenAI models:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Default, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum ImageModel {
    #[serde(rename = "gpt-image-1")]
    GptImage1,
    #[serde(rename = "gpt-image-1.5")]
    GptImage1dot5,
    #[serde(rename = "gpt-image-1-mini")]
    GptImage1Mini,
    #[default]
    #[serde(rename = "dall-e-2")]
    DallE2,
    #[serde(rename = "dall-e-3")]
    DallE3,
    #[serde(untagged)]
    Other(String),
```

Thanks for answer!

title: FunctionCallStream serializes None fields as JSON null instead of omitting them
author: pennycoders
body:
[`FunctionCallStream`](https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/main/async-openai/src/types/chat/chat_.rs#L1108-L1117) does not apply `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` to its two `Option<String>` fields (`name` and `arguments`), so a value constructed with `None` serializes as `"name": null` / `"arguments": null` rather than the field being absent.

This is inconsistent with the rest of `chat_.rs`, where neighbouring `Option<…>` fields use the attribute — e.g. [`service_tier`](https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/main/async-openai/src/types/chat/chat_.rs#L1088) and [`system_fingerprint`](https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/main/async-openai/src/types/chat/chat_.rs#L1092) a few lines earlier. 64 of the ~90 `Option<…>` fields in the file already use the attribute.

### Impact

Downstream OpenAI-shape consumers that read `function.arguments` with a string default (e.g. `dict.get('arguments', '')` in Python) get `None`/`null` back instead of `''`, and follow-up operations like `len(value)` or string concatenation then raise `TypeError`. The OpenAI streaming reference emits `""` on the first tool_call chunk and the [streaming-events schema](https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/resources/chat/subresources/completions/streaming-events) presents `arguments` as `optional string` — either an empty string or an absent field is wire-conformant; explicit `null` is not.

### Proposed fix

Add `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` to both fields. Two-line patch + a parallel test in `tests/ser_de.rs` mirroring the existing [`stream_options_none_fields_not_serialized`](https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/main/async-openai/tests/ser_de.rs#L30).

### Related (downstream)

This is the upstream root cause for agentgateway/agentgateway#1988 (originally proposed locally as agentgateway/agentgateway#1989, closed in favour of fixing here).

title: spec: transition from stainless back to openai-openapi

body:
Looks like OpenAI has transitioned away from Stainless here https://github.com/openai/openai-openapi

May need some investigation on new source of truth because https://app.stainless.com/api/spec/documented/openai/openapi.documented.yml and https://github.com/openai/openai-openapi/blob/master/openapi.yaml seem to differ. 


- use the new filename openapi.yaml instead of openapi.documented.yml
- update AGENTS.md 
- and  run first spec sync with new one

title: Title: create_stream* methods return Ok(stream) on HTTP error; error surfaces as first stream item instead of as Err from the call
author: max-zheng

title: Some fields are stricter then the openapi spec
author: Paul-E
body:
The spec defines `ResponseTextParam` and `TextResponseFormatConfiguration` as

```yaml
ResponseTextParam:
  type: object
  description: |
    Configuration options for a text response from the model. Can be plain                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
    text or structured JSON data. Learn more:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
    - [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
    - [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs)
  properties:
    format:
      $ref: '#/components/schemas/TextResponseFormatConfiguration'
    verbosity:
      $ref: '#/components/schemas/Verbosity'

TextResponseFormatConfiguration:
  description: >
    An object specifying the format that the model must output.
    
    
    Configuring `{ "type": "json_schema" }` enables Structured Outputs, 
    
    which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON schema. Learn more
    in the 
    
    [Structured Outputs guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).
    
    
    The default format is `{ "type": "text" }` with no additional options.
    
    
    **Not recommended for gpt-4o and newer models:**
    
    
    Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode,
    which
    
    ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using
    `json_schema`
    
    is preferred for models that support it.
  oneOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/ResponseFormatText'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/TextResponseFormatJsonSchema'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/ResponseFormatJsonObject'
```

The spec does not require `format`, but the definition of [`ResponseTextParam`](https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/main/async-openai/src/types/responses/response.rs#L1128), requires it and `TextResponseFormatConfiguration` has no default. A more spec compliant definition would define `TextResponseFormatConfiguration::Text` as default, and `ResponseTextParam.format` would use the `#[serde(default)]` annotion.

title: Preserve upstream HTTP status code in `OpenAIError` for non-2xx responses
author: max-zheng
body:
## Summary

  When an upstream HTTP response is not 2xx, `read_response` ([`src/client.rs#L726-L749`](https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/main/src/client.rs)) extracts the `ApiError` body but drops the original `reqwest::StatusCode`. Callers receive `OpenAIError::ApiError(ApiError { message, r#type, param, code })` with no way to know whether the upstream returned 400, 403, 422, 500, etc. — the only signal is whatever happens to be in the body, which varies wildly across OpenAI-compatible servers (sglang, vLLM, Modal-hosted endpoints, in-house gateways, etc.).

  ## Reproducer

  Any OpenAI-compatible upstream that returns a 4xx whose error body does **not** populate `type` or `code` in the way OpenAI itself does. Example body we see from one of our inference servers on a validation failure:

  ```json
  {
    "error": {
      "message": "Generation failed: temperature must be non-negative, got -1.0.",
      "type": "internal_error",
      "param": null,
      "code": null
    }
  }
  ```

  Whether the server returned this with HTTP 400 or 500, the caller gets the same `ApiError`. We can't route the response back to the client with the correct status because the status is gone.

  ## Why this matters

  We use async-openai inside a gateway service that proxies requests to multiple upstream model providers. When inference returns a 400, we want to surface a 400 to our client (it's their bad request); when inference returns a 500, we want to surface a 500. Today we have to guess by inspecting `ApiError::r#type` against a hand-maintained allowlist of strings (`invalid_request_error` → 400, `authentication_error` → 401, …) and treat anything unrecognized as 500. This is brittle and loses information for any upstream that doesn't follow OpenAI's exact `type` taxonomy.

  Specifically:
  - For 5xx (`read_response` lines 731-741), the body might not even be JSON, so `ApiError` is synthesized with `type: None, code: None` — fully stripping the status.
  - For non-2xx-non-5xx (`read_response` lines 744-748), the body is parsed but the `response.status()` value at line 727 is thrown away.

  ## Proposed change

  When the error originates from a real HTTP exchange, propagate the upstream `StatusCode` through to the caller in some form, so consumers don't have to reconstruct it by string-matching on `type`/`code`. I don't want to prescribe a specific shape (field on `ApiError`, new `OpenAIError` variant, accessor method, etc.) — happy to defer to whatever fits the crate's existing conventions and back-compat policy.

  Happy to send a PR once there's agreement on the shape.

title: Responses stream fails to deserialize in-progress web_search_call without `action`
author: rijkvanzanten

title: Allow reqwest 0.13's `rustls-no-provider` feature
author: valkum
body:
The update to `reqwest` 0.13 causes some trouble if you want to rely on `ring` instead of `aws-ls-rs`.

See https://github.com/seanmonstar/reqwest/issues/2924. You might also want to reach out here: https://github.com/seanmonstar/reqwest/issues/2871 

I am still not sure what the best way for libraries is with `reqwest` 0.13. Nevertheless, unless there is a better and documented recommendation, async-openai should offer a way to enable `reqwest/rustls-no-provider` instead of `reqwest/rustls`

title: Multimodal Responses payload without `detail` fails to deserialize (InputImageContent.detail missing `#[serde(default)]`)
author: ajcasagrande

title: bug: [DONE] SSE marker fails deserialization in stream() after v0.36.0 refactor
author: Linuxdazhao
body:
## Description

In v0.36.0 (PR #537), the streaming pipeline was refactored to remove the `reqwest-eventsource` dependency. As part of this change, the `stream()` function was rewritten to delegate to `stream_mapped_raw_events`.

The old `stream()` function (v0.36.1) checked for the `[DONE]` SSE end-of-stream marker **before** passing the event data to `serde_json::from_str`:

https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/v0.36.1/src/client.rs#L708-L716
```rust
if event.data == "[DONE]" {
    break;  // ← exits before deserialization
}
let response = serde_json::from_str::<O>(&event.data)...;
```

The new `stream()` function (v0.38.0) delegates to `stream_mapped_raw_events`, which checks `[DONE]` **after** calling the `event_mapper` closure:

https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/v0.38.0/src/client.rs#L814-L821
```rust
let done = event.data == "[DONE]";  // ← flag is stored
let response = event_mapper(event); // ← deserialization happens BEFORE done check
return Some((response, (event_stream, event_mapper, done)));
```

Since the `event_mapper` for `stream()` is `|event| serde_json::from_str::<O>(&event.data)`, the `[DONE]` string gets passed directly to the JSON parser, resulting in:

```
failed deserialization of: [DONE]
error: expected value at line 1 column 2
```

This affects both the WASM and non-WASM implementations of `stream_mapped_raw_events`.

## Expected Behavior

`[DONE]` is a valid SSE protocol-level end-of-stream marker. It should terminate the stream cleanly without being passed to the deserializer.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Use `async-openai` v0.38.0
2. Call `Chat::create_stream_byot()` with any custom deserialized type
3. The stream will produce an error when the server sends `data: [DONE]`

## Proposed Fix

Move the `[DONE]` check before the `event_mapper` call in both implementations of `stream_mapped_raw_events`, restoring the behavior from v0.36.1.

Happy to submit a PR if this looks correct.

title: `schema` must be present and not `null` when `text.format.type` is `json_schema`
author: nascent-david
body:
This is used to enable [structured model outputs](https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/resources/responses/methods/create#(resource)%20responses%20%3E%20(model)%20response_text_config%20%3E%20(schema)%20%3E%20(property)%20format%20%2B%20(resource)%20responses%20%3E%20(model)%20response_format_text_json_schema_config%20%3E%20(schema)%20%3E%20(property)%20schema).

[`schema` in `async_openai::types::assistants::ResponseFormatJsonSchema`](https://docs.rs/async-openai/0.34.0/src/async_openai/types/shared/response_format.rs.html#25-26) is an `Option` (which is skipped if `None`), but this contradicts [OpenAI's official type from the Python SDK](https://github.com/openai/openai-python/blob/58184ad545ee2abd98e171ee09766f259d7f38cd/src/openai/types/responses/response_format_text_json_schema_config.py#L27), where it is _not_ `Optional`.

OpenAI's API is accommodating and will fall back to disabling structured outputs if you do not provide a `schema`, but other implementations, [like VLLM](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/7e76af14fabfc72144c627331b87d581df361158/vllm/entrypoints/openai/responses/protocol.py#L166), are not, and will reject your request:

```json
{
'type': 'missing', 'loc': ('body', 'text', 'format', 'ResponseFormatTextJSONSchemaConfig', 'schema'),
   'msg': 'Field required',
...
```

title: Missing type field from ResponseReasoningItem.content
author: ishbir
body:
Hello,

Looking at the official API docs: https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/resources/responses#(resource)%20responses%20%3E%20(model)%20response_input_item%20%3E%20(schema) `ResponseReasoningItem.content` has a `type` field, but this crate only has a String for the reasoning content. This causes issues with some non-OpenAI APIs which try to stay faithful to the OpenAI API and expect "reasoning_text" as the type.

I can work around this locally but thought I would report this so that it gets fixed in the library.

Thanks,
Ishbir Singh

title: ChatCompletionStreamOptions.include_obfuscation should be skipped during serialization if none
author: codito
body: Please consider marking `ChatCompletionStreamOptions.include_obfuscation` as `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. As per [spec](https://github.com/64bit/async-openai/blob/37f17d4dfe898dc4be36048365701f267459cb83/openapi.documented.yml) (screenshot below), it is optional field. Including this field even when none breaks the Gemini's OpenAI compatible API. async-openai version: v0.32.2
